issues.detail: Verified the federate-vs-Workload seam pointer is present and correctly drawn: the Federate bullet defines the operator identity as the Workforce-federated principal:// subject and explicitly distinguishes it from the envoy's Workload 'federates-to-AWS' egress, citing both RBSHR Envoy and memo-20260527's Workforce-vs-Workload table as the existing separation. This is the high-risk nuance (two distinct uses of 'federate') and it lands accurately.

Verified the deferral-honoring breadcrumb is present in memo-20260527 immediately after its bolded Status sentence and before the --- divider (so it is within the Status block). It points to the 605 citizen memo, flags the one-SA-per-role and verb-naming assumptions as possibly changing, states divergences fold in once the model is frozen, and ends 'Not yet revised.' The 605 memo's own closing claim (line 336: 'A deferral-honoring breadcrumb has been added to memo-20260527's Status block now') is consistent with what is actually on disk in the 527 memo.

COVERAGE check of findings 1-11 against on-disk files: all 11 are present and substantively correct (not merely gestured at). The sweep is committed (1c93eb221, heat ₣BZ); working tree clean. Per-finding verification: (1) Citizen-naming-migration section present, memo lines 284-306; identity-only SA names ('alice' not 'director-alice', line 287); enumerates RBSDK/RBSRK (line 291), RBSDD (292), RBSDR (293), RBSRA/RBSRR (294); delete+recreate cutover routed through rbk-08 revoke layer + Class-C tolerance (lines 296-298). (2) Member-first audit axis present (lines 162-169, 191-193); audit-domain line corrected to two axes (lines 191-193); repoAdmin 'covers' claim softened to conditional 'covered ONLY once this axis exists' (line 169). (3) Divest/remove = withdraw ledger intent FIRST, then revoke IAM, then delete SA, with invariant 'no IAM revoke without a prior ledger withdrawal' (lines 80-84). (4) RBSHR reword verified in commit diff: both artifact-'citizen' occurrences (Hoard entry line 97, Bullion entry line 222) -> 'holding'; exactly 2 prose edits as expected; memo note recording the supersede present (lines 19-21, 'Mints over RBSHR's prose use of citizen for a depot artifact'). (5) 'declared roster' -> 'declared ledger' applied throughout; 'roster' correctly preserved ONLY for the actual-reading RBSDR cult-verb (lines 31-33, 57, 293, 301); audit = diff(actual-state roster, declared ledger) stated at lines 32-33. (6) Capability-set definition EXPANSION gated behind human adjudication, distinguished from per-identity failed-grant auto-heal (lines 179-185); 'who may edit capability-set definitions is a first-class authority' (lines 184-185). (7) 10-keys-per-SA hard quota; rekey order create-new -> deliver RBRA -> verify -> delete-old; reclaim stale keys (lines 74-79, 315). (8) Concurrency: auto-converge re-reads ledger under etag immediately before each grant; both teardown sides write the ledger first (lines 186-190). (9) Half-failed revoke handled as ours via idempotent retry + recorded 'revoking' intent marker, not adversarial surplus (lines 138-144); correction 'the AUDIT never auto-revokes' with divest/remove carve-out (lines 152-154). (10) Orphan sweep is integrity-advisory not authoritative; authoritative signal = USER_MANAGED key on an SA absent from the declared ledger (lines 200-204). (11) Ledger home explicitly disqualifies the build bucket because director holds roles/storage.objectCreator there; write-ACL reachable by no capability-set, governor/payor only; cinch 'ledger-write authority is never a capability-set member' (lines 109-117).

The sweep's roster-vs-ledger disambiguation broke this table row internally. The row reads: `| roster (verb) | read the declared ledger | the actual-reading verb; the audit diffs it against IAM |`. The 'Decomposes to' cell ('read the declared ledger') directly contradicts its own Notes cell ('the actual-reading verb; the audit diffs it against IAM') — one says it reads stored intent, the other says it reads actual IAM state. It also contradicts the vocabulary definition the same sweep wrote at lines 31-33, which reserves the `roster` cult-verb (RBSDR) for reading the *actual* discovered IAM state, NOT the declared ledger. The pre-sweep row was `| roster | read the declared roster | |`; the rename should have flipped the decomposition to the actual-state read, since reading the declared ledger is the separate `ledger-read` verb named at line 62 ('grant/revoke/ledger-read'). As written, two distinct verb names ('roster' here, 'ledger-read' at line 62) both claim to read the declared ledger while the vocabulary says roster reads actual state.
issues.fix: Change the 'Decomposes to' cell from 'read the declared ledger' to the actual-state read (e.g. 'read the actual IAM state'), so it matches the Notes cell ('the actual-reading verb') and the vocabulary at lines 31-33. The declared-ledger read is already covered by 'ledger-read' (line 58/62).

issues.detail: The divest row decomposes to 'revoke(all held) + remove-citizen' — listing revoke BEFORE remove-citizen. But the same sweep added the invariant 'no IAM revoke without a prior ledger withdrawal' (line 81) and defined remove-citizen as ledger-withdraw-FIRST then revoke IAM (lines 80-81). Since remove-citizen is the step that performs the ledger withdrawal, listing it after revoke(all held) inverts the ledger-first ordering the invariant demands. The Notes cell 'ledger-withdraw-first (see Identity verbs)' flags the correct intent and the cross-reference partially rescues it, but the literal sequence in the 'Decomposes to' cell still reads revoke-before-ledger-withdraw, contradicting the invariant.
issues.fix: Reorder the decomposition to surface the ledger-first sequence, e.g. 'withdraw-ledger-intent + revoke(all held) + delete-SA', or annotate that remove-citizen's ledger-withdraw precedes the revoke. The Notes already say 'ledger-withdraw-first'; make the 'Decomposes to' cell not contradict it.

issues.detail: Checked the high-risk axis 'the audit never auto-revokes' vs divest legitimately revoking. The text at 152-154 reads '**The *audit* never auto-revokes** ... (Divest/remove *do* revoke; that is an explicit operator teardown, not an audit heal.)' This is internally consistent and consistent with the divest/remove verb semantics elsewhere (lines 56, 80, 154). The old broken 'No verb auto-revokes' claim was correctly narrowed to 'the audit never auto-revokes'. No regression here.

Checked the member-first-axis vs audit-domain-line consistency. The member-first axis (162-169), the 'Audit domain' bullet (191-193, 'A non-ledger, non-system member is flagged, not ignored'), and the repoAdmin residual claim (169 'covered only once this axis exists'; 232-235 'covered by the audit's member-first axis') are mutually consistent. The old contradictory 'non-roster principals are out of scope' line and the unsoftened repoAdmin 'covers' claim were both corrected. No regression.

The deferral-honoring breadcrumb landed correctly and is the high-risk item for this check. It sits immediately after the Status block (near Status, before the --- divider), names heat ₣BZ, names the citizen memo by path (Memos/memo-20260605-citizen-capability-model.md), and explicitly honors the 'record when frozen' deferral: 'that memo will fold its divergences in once the model is frozen. Not yet revised.' It is a pointer, NOT the full supersede note — it does not enumerate the one-SA-per-role / verb-naming divergences. This exactly matches the citizen memo's own statement (lines 336-337) that 'A deferral-honoring breadcrumb has been added to memo-20260527's Status block now; the full divergence note folds in when the model is frozen.' Checked clean.

Cross-doc check 1: the memo's 'supersedes RBSHR prose citizen' note matches the actual RBSHR rewording. RBSHR has ZERO remaining 'citizen' references (grep -i citizen returns nothing). The Hoard entry (RBSHR line 94-99) now reads 'A {rbtgi_depot} holding alongside the {rbtga_ark}' — the depot-artifact use that was previously 'citizen' is now 'holding'. The citizen memo records this in two consistent places: inline mint note (lines 19-21, 'Mints over RBSHR''s prose use of "citizen" for a depot *artifact* — those are reworded to "holding"') and the Holdings definition (lines 34-35, 'Holdings — the depot''s artifacts (arks/hoards/bullions)'). RBSHR''s holding-family usage (line 97 hoard, line 222 bullion) is consistent with the memo''s arks/hoards/bullions enumeration. Checked clean.

All expected changes verified as present and correct. Key changes include: (1) SA naming migration to identity-only (RBSDK/RBSRK/RBSDD/RBSDR/RBSRA surfaces documented), (2) member-first audit axis with enumeration of actual members on setIamPolicy-reachable resources, (3) divest/remove verb with ledger-withdrawal-first invariant, (5) 'declared ledger' terminology consistently replacing 'declared roster' (used only for actual-reading verb), (6) capability-set definition expansion gated behind human adjudication with 'who edits definitions' as first-class authority, (7) 10-keys-per-SA quota with rekey order (create-new→deliver→verify→delete-old), (8) concurrency with etag-re-read before each grant and teardown ledger-first writes, (9) half-failed revoke handling with revoking-marker and audit never auto-revokes, (10) orphan sweep as integrity-advisory with USER_MANAGED key absence from ledger as authoritative signal, (11) ledger home NOT build bucket with cinch that ledger-write is never a capability-set member, (12) Writers topology-conditional rule, (13) mantle-not-special caveat, (14-15) migration as one-time name-based bootstrap with orphan marker stamp, (16) ephemeral-governor caveat with key-destruction coupling, (20) federate vs Workload distinction clearly marked.
issues.fix: No changes needed. This memo has been correctly updated with all expected changes from the edit sweep.

issues.detail: Prose edits correctly applied. Hoard line 97: 'holding alongside the {rbtga_ark}' — 'citizen' terminology for depot artifact correctly replaced with 'holding'. Bullion line 222: 'bullions are the new AWS-side holding with S3-native retention semantics' — correctly uses 'holding' instead of any artifact-identity naming. No stray 'citizen' artifact references remain.
issues.fix: No changes needed. RBSHR prose edits are correct.

NOTE (2026-06-05) records that the keyfile-tier shape is under active redesign in heat BZ (memo-20260605-citizen-capability-model.md), explicitly noting that one-SA-per-role and verb-naming assumptions 'may change' and that full divergence will be folded in once the model is frozen. This breadcrumb correctly honors the deferral and signals the reader to the superseding citizen model memo.
issues.fix: No changes needed. Breadcrumb is properly placed and formatted.

residuals.issue: The roster row's 'Decomposes to' cell ('read the declared ledger') contradicts its own Notes cell ('the actual-reading verb; the audit diffs it against IAM') and the vocabulary the same sweep wrote at lines 31-33, which reserves the `roster` cult-verb (RBSDR) for reading the *actual* discovered IAM state, NOT the declared ledger. As written, 'roster' here and 'ledger-read' at line 62 both claim to read the declared ledger, while the audit (lines 32-33) is defined as the diff between the actual-state roster and the declared ledger — so roster must read actual state. Verified against the file.
residuals.fix: Change the 'Decomposes to' cell on line 57 from 'read the declared ledger' to the actual-state read (e.g. 'read the actual IAM state'), so it matches the Notes cell ('the actual-reading verb') and the vocabulary at lines 31-33. The declared-ledger read is already covered by 'ledger-read' at line 62.

residuals.issue: The divest row's 'Decomposes to' cell reads 'revoke(all held) + remove-citizen', listing revoke BEFORE remove-citizen. But remove-citizen is defined (lines 80-81) as ledger-withdraw-FIRST, then revoke IAM, under the invariant 'no IAM revoke without a prior ledger withdrawal'. The literal left-to-right sequence inverts that ordering. The Notes cell ('ledger-withdraw-first (see Identity verbs)') and the cross-reference only partially rescue it; the literal sequence in the 'Decomposes to' cell still reads revoke-before-ledger-withdraw. Verified against the file.
residuals.fix: Reorder the decomposition on line 56 to surface the ledger-first sequence (e.g. 'withdraw-ledger-intent + revoke(all held) + delete-SA'), or annotate that remove-citizen's ledger-withdraw precedes the revoke, so the 'Decomposes to' cell stops contradicting its own 'ledger-withdraw-first' Note.

result.summary: Verified both findings against Memos/memo-20260605-citizen-capability-model.md. Both are real, distinct (separate table rows, separate defects), non-duplicative residuals from the roster-vs-ledger disambiguation sweep, and each carries a concrete fix. No false alarms to drop. (1) Line 57 (roster row, high): the 'Decomposes to' cell says roster reads the declared ledger, contradicting both its own Notes cell and the lines 31-33 vocabulary that reserves roster for the actual IAM-state read — and duplicating the 'ledger-read' verb at line 62. (2) Line 56 (divest row, medium): the decomposition lists revoke before remove-citizen, inverting the ledger-withdraw-first ordering and the 'no IAM revoke without a prior ledger withdrawal' invariant at lines 80-81; the Notes cross-reference only partially rescues the literal sequence.
